methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $7M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Terranova is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Laurence Allen that has raised $7M to date. The robotics and manufacturing sector includes 9 identified private peers and 1 public comparable, though peer valuation data proved insufficient for reliable median benchmarking at the seed stage within this subsector.

Stage-progression analysis, the primary applicable method, uses the $7M raised as a value proxy and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $18M. Peer-median methodology was not applicable due to insufficient comparable datapoints meeting narrow stage and tier filters within robotics and manufacturing; the 11 same-sector peers identified do not yield the minimum three comparable observations needed to establish a reliable median, and broadening to all-sector peers risked introducing sector-specific noise.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $15M (low) to $20M (high), with a median point of $18M. This range reflects a cautious single-method triangulation. Key uncertainties include the absence of proximate peer validation—reliance on stage-progression mechanics alone introduces execution risk typical of seed-stage startups—and the limited public and private comparable universe in this subsector, which constrains benchmarking rigor. Further traction data or identified peers would improve conviction.
